text: In this video we would like to present fistulotomy for intersphincteric anal fistula. We would like to start by reminding you of Park's classification of most common fistulas. and also the Goodsall's rule that states if the external opening is anterior to the line, the fistula usually runs directly into the anal canal, and if the external opening is posterior to the line, the fistula usually curves to the posterior midline of the canal.

A forty-seven-year-old gentleman presented at our clinic complaining of constant purulent secretion through the opening right posterior from anus. MRI was performed. It showed intersphincteric posterior fistula. According to Park's classification, the fistula in our case was intersphincteric fistula with simple low track.

The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position with the buttocks taped aside. The surgeon injects normal saline into the external fistula opening to confirm the presence of an internal opening.
The surgeon probes the intersphincteric anal fistula, followed by performing a fistulotomy to open and expose the fistula tract.
The surgeon performs curettage of the opened fistula tract, followed by the insertion of a hemostatic sponge into the anal canal.
